subagent_reports.topic: Audited completed-workflow recovery for adv_change_close and adv_change_bulk_close

sources.label: Archive recovery precedent (canonical reference pattern)
sources.summary: adv_change_archive already implements the exact proposed pattern: signal/save first, on failure check isWorkflowCompletedError + poisoned probe under recoveryMode='poisoned_history', then saveRecoveredChangeStatus with recovery_audit (reason+evidence) and returns _recoveryMutation:true + RECOVERY_RECONCILIATION_WARNING.

sources.label: Disk-projection recovery writer (status transition)
sources.summary: saveRecoveredChangeStatus(status) exists, bypasses store.changes.save (which would re-route through workflow), writes disk via saveChange, structurally requires RecoveryWriteAuthorization{reason,evidence}. Status type is Change['status'] — supports 'closed'/'cancelled' values as defined by the Change type.

sources.label: Recovery classification helpers
sources.summary: isWorkflowCompletedError (exact name set + message substring patterns, false-positive-safe) and isPreciseWorkflowRecoveryEvidence (poisoned OR completed evidence regex) both exist and are workflow-safe. RECOVERY_RECONCILIATION_WARNING constant available.

sources.label: Current close handler — recovery gap confirmed
sources.summary: adv_change_close only calls fireSignalAndRefresh(changeCancelledSignal); catch block returns raw error + contextMismatch. No completed-workflow fallback. Dry-run (3026-3035) already no-write.

sources.label: Current bulk-close handler — recovery gap confirmed
sources.summary: Per-id loop fires changeCancelledSignal, per-id catch records {success:false,error}. No recovery branch. Disk sweep only runs for successful ids (3245-3255) — partial-failure rollback preserved. Dry-run (3172-3187) already no-write.

sources.label: Schema/evidence-gating precedent (contract + task tools)
sources.summary: recoveryMode enum + recoveryEvidence schema with isPreciseWorkflowRecoveryEvidence validation is the established cross-tool convention; close/bulk-close should mirror it verbatim (RecoveryModeSchema).

sources.label: Temporal recovery doctrine
sources.summary: Recovery writes must not refresh Temporal cache (completed-but-queryable workflow projects stale state back over disk repair). Recovery is explicit/audited/last-resort only.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Existing pattern: adv_change_archive, adv_contract_*, and task mutation tools already implement signal-first → on-failure completed/poisoned classification → authorized disk-projection write → audit + reconciliation warning. Reference pattern: identical. The proposed close/bulk-close design is a direct, faithful replication of a shipped, tested precedent (lines 3920-4009) reusing the SAME helpers (saveRecoveredChangeStatus, isWorkflowCompletedError, isPreciseWorkflowRecoveryEvidence, RECOVERY_RECONCILIATION_WARNING). Deviation from canonical ADV recovery architecture: NONE. This is the boring, proven path — no novel mechanism introduced.

validation.notes: Design is VALIDATED. All four design assertions verified against source: (1) recoveryMode/recoveryEvidence schema matches contract.ts/task.ts convention; (2) precise-evidence validation helper exists and is reused; (3) saveRecoveredChangeStatus(status:'closed') exists, bypasses workflow re-routing, requires audit; (4) dry-run already no-write in both handlers. No CONFLICT.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Proceed. Implement by mirroring archive recovery (change.ts:3936-4009) exactly: signal first; on catch, gate on recoveryMode==='poisoned_history'; classify via isWorkflowCompletedError(error) (completed) OR workflowHasPoisonedRecoveryEvidence probe (poisoned); only then call saveRecoveredChangeStatus({status, authorization:{reason: completedWorkflow?'completed_workflow_close_recovery':'poisoned_history_close_recovery', evidence: recoveryEvidence ?? errorText}}); return _recoveryMutation:true + RECOVERY_RECONCILIATION_WARNING; run best-effort removeChangeDir/sweep AFTER recovery succeeds. Use RecoveryModeSchema from task.ts for schema parity. Validate recoveryEvidence with isPreciseWorkflowRecoveryEvidence when recoveryMode set, before the try block (matches archive 3442-3452).
follow_ups: RISK (low): saveRecoveredChangeStatus deliberately does NOT bestEffortRefresh (unlike task writers) — confirm close recovery path does not invalidate cache afterward, else stale completed-workflow state reprojects over the disk close (per _recovery-writers.ts:12-17 and 158-172). Archive path correctly avoids refresh; copy that exactly.

RISK (bulk, medium): in bulk close the recovery branch must live INSIDE the per-id try/catch loop (3210-3237), not the outer try. Each id classifies its own error independently; a completed-workflow id recovers while a genuinely-failing id still records {success:false}. Do not let one recovery short-circuit the loop.
BULK ordering: ensure disk sweep (3245-3255) treats recovery-closed ids as successfulIds so changes/<id> dirs are removed; otherwise recovered closes leak source dirs.
DENY-BY-DEFAULT: verify a completed-workflow close failure WITHOUT recoveryMode still returns the raw error (negative test) — mirror archive test change.test.ts:1478-1506.

TEST PARITY: add single-close recovery (completed + poisoned-evidence variants), bulk mixed (one recovers, one hard-fails), and no-recoveryMode negative — mirroring change.test.ts:1442-1545 and task.test.ts:585-690.

Confirm Change['status'] union includes the intended terminal close value ('closed' vs 'cancelled') so saveRecoveredChangeStatus type-checks; close currently fires changeCancelledSignal so the disk status value must match what the cancelled signal would have set.

documents.proposal:
# Fix change close recovery

## Problem

`adv_change_close` and `adv_change_bulk_close` fail with `workflow execution already completed` when a draft/active change projection still exists but its Temporal workflow is already terminal. This blocks legitimate user-approved cancellation/removal of stale changes such as `renameAdvWorktreeNamespace`.

## Direction

Add an audited recovery path to the close tools, mirroring the existing archive/gate poisoned-history recovery pattern: normal close still signals Temporal; completed-workflow recovery requires explicit recovery mode and evidence, then applies a disk-projection close/remove only when the live signal path fails because the workflow is already completed.

## Success Criteria

- `adv_change_close` accepts an explicit recovery mode for completed/poisoned workflow close repair.
- `adv_change_bulk_close` supports the same recovery path for explicit/filter closures.
- Recovery requires user approval evidence and precise completed-workflow evidence.
- Tests prove normal close remains signal-first and recovery only activates for completed-workflow failures.
- After deploy/restart, the stale `renameAdvWorktreeNamespace` change can be closed with audited evidence.

## Scope

- `plugin/src/tools/change.ts` close and bulk close tool schemas/handlers.
- `plugin/src/tools/_recovery-writers.ts` if terminal close projection needs an audited helper beyond `saveRecoveredChangeStatus`.
- `plugin/src/tools/change.test.ts` and preflight/schema tests as needed.
- `plugin/src/utils/tool-arg-preflight.ts` only if strict-mode placeholder policy needs new recovery fields.

## Error Handling

- Without recovery mode, completed-workflow errors remain surfaced unchanged.
- With recovery mode but missing/imprecise evidence, the tool returns a structured error before mutating state.
- With recovery mode and non-completed workflow failure, the tool returns the original failure and does not write disk projection.
- Disk cleanup remains best-effort and reported as warning/failure data, not hidden.

## Out of Scope

- Manual state-file surgery.
- Changing archive recovery semantics.
- Changing unrelated gate/task recovery paths.
documents.problemStatement: Stale ADV changes can remain visible even when their backing Temporal workflow is already completed. Current close tools always signal the workflow, so user-approved close/removal fails with `workflow execution already completed` and there is no safe force-close path.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement

## Objectives

1. Add a safe, audited recovery close path for changes whose workflow is already completed.
2. Keep normal close behavior signal-first and unchanged.
3. Extend both single and bulk close surfaces consistently.
4. Provide tests for recovery validation and completed-workflow fallback.
5. Use the fixed tool to close `renameAdvWorktreeNamespace` once the live tool surface is available.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `adv_change_close` exposes recovery arguments and rejects recovery without precise evidence.
2. `adv_change_close` succeeds by disk projection only after the normal signal path fails with completed-workflow evidence.
3. `adv_change_bulk_close` passes recovery arguments through to every selected close and preserves dry-run behavior.
4. Existing normal close tests continue to prove signal-first behavior.
5. Targeted tests covering change close recovery pass.
6. The stale `renameAdvWorktreeNamespace` close is completed or the session reports the required restart/deploy blocker explicitly.

## Constraints

- Do not directly edit ADV state files by hand.
- Do not bypass user approval metadata.
- Do not make recovery the default path.
- Do not weaken protected/invalid target checks in bulk close.
documents.design:
# Design

## Evidence

- `docs/temporal-recovery.md` documents recovery as audited, explicit, and not a manual disk edit.
- `plugin/src/tools/_recovery-writers.ts` already contains disk-projection writers and states completed-workflow recovery must carry authorization reason/evidence.
- `plugin/src/temporal/recovery-classification.ts` already provides `isWorkflowCompletedError` and `isPreciseWorkflowRecoveryEvidence`.
- `plugin/src/tools/change.ts` close handlers currently catch and return the Temporal signal error without a recovery fallback.

## Implementation

- Extend `adv_change_close` and `adv_change_bulk_close` schemas with optional `recoveryMode: 'normal' | 'poisoned_history'` and `recoveryEvidence` to align existing recovery terminology.
- Validate: when `recoveryMode === 'poisoned_history'`, require non-empty precise workflow recovery evidence via `isPreciseWorkflowRecoveryEvidence`.
- Extract a close helper used by single and bulk paths:
  1. Try `changeCancelledSignal` via `fireSignalAndRefresh`.
  2. On success, remove/sweep closed change directory as today.
  3. On failure, if recovery mode is enabled and `isWorkflowCompletedError(error)` is true, call `saveRecoveredChangeStatus` with status `closed`, authorization reason `completed_workflow_close_recovery`, and recovery evidence.
  4. Then perform best-effort directory cleanup through existing removal/sweep helpers.
  5. If failure is not completed-workflow, return original error.
- Preserve dry-run: no signals, no disk writes, no cleanup.
- Add tests for rejection without evidence, no recovery without recovery mode, single close recovery success, and bulk close recovery success/dry-run.

## Runtime note

OpenCode caches deployed plugin tool schemas. After source fix is merged/deployed, a fresh OpenCode session is required before the new recovery arguments can be used through live `adv_change_close`.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

`adv_change_close` and `adv_change_bulk_close` now have an explicit, audited completed-workflow recovery path. Normal close remains signal-first. Recovery is opt-in via `recoveryMode: 'poisoned_history'`, requires precise `recoveryEvidence`, and only disk-projects a closed status after the normal signal path fails with completed-workflow evidence.

The bulk close path applies recovery per selected change without short-circuiting siblings, preserves dry-run no-write behavior, and treats recovery-closed items as successful for cleanup. Strict-mode placeholder policy now normalizes blank recovery fields for close tools while preserving non-blank approval evidence requirements.

Verification passed: targeted close/preflight tests, typecheck, lint, format check, and reviewer schemas check. The stale `renameAdvWorktreeNamespace` close cannot be completed from this current OpenCode session because the live tool schema is cached; after archive/deploy and a fresh OpenCode session, close it with the new recovery arguments and completed-workflow evidence.

# Acceptance

Reviewed at: 2026-06-06T20:34:15.628Z

## Contract Review Matrix

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| AC1 | acceptance_criterion | `adv_change_close` exposes recovery arguments and rejects recovery without precise evidence. | pass | `adv_change_close` schema includes optional `recoveryMode` and `recoveryEvidence`; handler validates precise recovery evidence when recoveryMode='poisoned_history'. |
| AC2 | acceptance_criterion | `adv_change_close` succeeds by disk projection only after the normal signal path fails with completed-workflow evidence. | pass | `change.test.ts` includes `recovers completed-workflow close failure with audited disk projection`, passing under `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/tools/change.test.ts src/utils/tool-arg-preflight.test.ts`. |
| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | `adv_change_bulk_close` passes recovery arguments through to every selected close and preserves dry-run behavior. | pass | `change.test.ts` includes `recovers completed-workflow failures per id during bulk close` and dry-run coverage remains passing. |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | Existing normal close tests continue to prove signal-first behavior. | pass | Existing normal close test `fires changeCancelledSignal with approval metadata` passed; recovery tests prove no recovery without recoveryMode. |
| AC5 | acceptance_criterion | Targeted tests covering change close recovery pass. | pass | Verification passed: targeted tests (150), typecheck, lint, format:check, reviewer ran schemas:check. |
| AC6 | acceptance_criterion | The stale `renameAdvWorktreeNamespace` close is completed or the session reports the required restart/deploy blocker explicitly. | pass | The stale close is not completed in this live session because OpenCode caches deployed plugin tool schemas; actual close requires build/deploy and fresh OpenCode session before new recovery args can be invoked. This explicitly satisfies the blocker branch of AC6. |
| C1 | constraint | Do not directly edit ADV state files by hand. | respected | No ADV state files edited directly; implementation uses `saveRecoveredChangeStatus` through audited recovery path only. |
| C2 | constraint | Do not bypass user approval metadata. | respected | Both close tools still require `approvedByUser: true` and non-blank `approvalEvidence`; recovery does not bypass that validation. |
| C3 | constraint | Do not make recovery the default path. | respected | Recovery activates only when `recoveryMode === 'poisoned_history'`; no recovery mode returns original completed-workflow error. |
| C4 | constraint | Do not weaken protected/invalid target checks in bulk close. | respected | Bulk close still resolves selection through existing protected/invalid target checks before dry-run or execution. |
